headline: fkwu DOES support float four-way; a recursive list-slicing/building helper returning a float sublist DIVERGES on fkwu (returns 0). Corrects the prior false claim 'fkwu lacks the float op family'.

float_is_supported.claim: fkwu computes fp64 four-way — proven by NON-TRIVIAL verdicts that require correct float math (a wrong float drops verdict bits).

genuine_four_way_bands.note: fsin/fcos/fln + Goertzel + mf-frame builds a float list; exact integer-eq checks
tn-erf + tn-gelu-erf scalar float, 8 exact checks

divergence.what: A recursive take/slice that returns a float SUBLIST evaluates to 0 on fkwu while Go/Rust/TS agree on the correct value.

minimal_repro.recipe: (defn f (n xs) (if (eq n 0) (empty) (cons (head xs) (f (sub n 1) (tail xs)))))

divergence.contrast: Byte-identical helpers under conv-stem's cv-take ALSO diverge when the actual value is printed (cv-conv value: go=-10000, fkwu=0). conv-stem's band verdict 1 was a FALSE PASS: broken slicing -> empty/zero output -> maxdiff 0 < tol -> verdict 1, coincidentally matching Go.
divergence.not_a_wall: This is a DIVERGENCE (one kernel computes a different answer), not a missing op FAMILY. Per fourth-arm-bands.txt rules it is a correctness bug to root-cause in the flattener/walker (form-flatten.fk / fkc-emit-universal), tracked here, never buried by omission.
divergence.methodological_note: Boolean-verdict bands can FALSE-PASS on fkwu when a divergence trivializes the comparison. A four-way verdict is only trustworthy when the verdict derives from the COMPUTED VALUE (exact-eq / bit-sum), as mel-frame and transformer-gelu-erf do.

status: owned, tracked to a flattener/walker fix; the slicing-dependent whisper-tiny bands stay three-way until it lands
